context.pr_title: feat: Switch to enum queue mode, add 'replace' mode.
context.pr_body:
Follow up to https://github.com/Acly/krita-ai-diffusion/pull/1879

Kind of janky because of the fact that job queue tasks are split up before the client- I make clear-queue a property of the first job.

Not sure if the enum is in the right place.

Tentatively planning to implement ctrl-click as "Alternate queue mode".

agents.name: analysis_agent
agents.system_prompt:
You are an Analysis Agent specialized in Pull Request analysis. ## Project Context (MASCA Analysis) ## Project Analysis ### README

Generative AI for Krita

✨[Features](#features) | ⭳ [Download](https://github.com/Acly/krita-ai-diffusion/releases/latest) | 🛠️[Installation](https://docs.interstice.cloud/installation) | 🎞️ [Video](https://youtu.be/Ly6USRwTHe0) | 🖼️[Gallery](#gallery) | 📖[User Guide](https://docs.interstice.cloud) | 💬[Discussion](https://github.com/Acly/krita-ai-diffusion/discussions) | 🗣️[Discord](https://discord.gg/pWyzHfHHhU) This is a plugin to use generative AI in image painting and editing workflows from within Krita. Visit [**www.interstice.cloud**](https://www.interstice.cloud) for an introduction. Learn how to install and use it on [**docs.interstice.cloud**](https://docs.interstice.cloud). The main goals of this project are: * **Precision and Control.** Creating entire images from text can be unpredictable. To get the result you envision, you can restrict generation to selections, refine existing content with a variable degree of strength, focus text on image regions, and guide generation with reference images, sketches, line art, depth maps, and more. * **Workflow Integration.** Most image generation tools focus heavily on AI parameters. This project aims to be an unobtrusive tool that integrates and synergizes with image editing workflows in Krita. Draw, paint, edit and generate seamlessly without worrying about resolution and technical details. * **Local, Open, Free.** We are committed to open source models. Customize presets, bring your own models, and run everything local on your hardware. Cloud generation is also available to get started quickly without heavy investment. [![Watch video demo](media/screenshot-video-preview.webp)](https://youtu.be/Ly6USRwTHe0 "Watch video demo") ## Features * **Inpainting**: Use selections for generative fill, expand, to add or remove objects * **Live Painting**: Let AI interpret your canvas in real time for immediate feedback. [Watch Video](https://youtu.be/AF2VyqSApjA?si=Ve5uQJWcNOATtABU) * **Upscaling**: Upscale and enrich images to 4k, 8k and beyond without running out of memory. * **Diffusion Models**: Supports Stable Diffusion 1.5, and XL. Partial support for [Flux](https://github.com/Acly/krita-ai-diffusion/discussions/1176) and SD3. * **ControlNet**: Scribble, Line art, Canny edge, Pose, Depth, Normals, Segmentation, +more * **IP-Adapter**: Reference images, Style and composition transfer, Face swap * **Regions**: Assign individual text descriptions to image areas defined by layers. * **Job Queue**: Queue and cancel generation jobs while working on your image. * **History**: Preview results and browse previous generations and prompts at any time. * **Strong Defaults**: Versatile default style presets allow for a streamlined UI. * **Customization**: Create your own presets - custom checkpoints, LoRA, samplers and more. ## Getting Started See the [Plugin Install ## Your Task When you receive a PR title and body, you must: 1. **Analyze the request**: Understand what the PR wants to achieve 2. **Explore the code**: Use tools to navigate base_project and find relevant files/functions 3. **Identify targets**: Determine exactly which files and functions need to be modified 4. **Optimize MASCA**: Produce a version of the MASCA analysis focused on the specific PR ## Available Tools - `read_base_project_file(file_path)`: Read a file from the project - `list_base_project_directory(directory, pattern, recursive)`: List files/folders ## Required Output You must produce a structured output with: - `pr_title`: PR title - `pr_body`: PR body - `masca_optimized`: MASCA optimized for this specific PR - `files_to_modify`: List of files to modify with motivation - `functions_to_modify`: List of functions to modify with motivation - `analysis_summary`: Summary of your analysis ## Guidelines - Explore the code before drawing conclusions - Search for files with names relevant to the PR - Identify specific functions, not just generic files - Be precise with paths (relative to base_project) - For function names use the format "ClassName.method_name" for methods
agents.input_prompt:
Analyze this Pull Request:

## Title
feat: Switch to enum queue mode, add 'replace' mode.

## Description
Follow up to https://github.com/Acly/krita-ai-diffusion/pull/1879

Kind of janky because of the fact that job queue tasks are split up before the client- I make clear-queue a property of the first job.

Not sure if the enum is in the right place.

Tentatively planning to implement ctrl-click as "Alternate queue mode".

Explore the source code to identify the files and functions to modify.

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
This file defines the client-side data model and abstract Client API for interacting with a diffusion server, plus utilities for resolving architectures and validating/uploading model dependencies. It also contains message/event types used for job queue notifications and status updates.

### Architecture
- Top-level enums/NamedTuples describe events and message payloads used by the client-server protocol (e.g., `ClientEvent`, `ClientMessage`, `TextOutput`, `SharedWorkflow`).
- `Client` is an abstract base class that defines the public client API (connect, enqueue, listen, interrupt, clear_queue, etc.). Concrete client implementations must implement queue-related behavior.
- Model/resource management is handled by `ClientModels` and `ModelDict`, which map resource identifiers to available models and provide architecture-aware lookups.
- Utility functions `resolve_arch`, `filter_supported_styles`, and `loras_to_upload` operate on `Style`, `WorkflowInput`, and `ClientModels` to determine compatible architectures and which Lora files must be uploaded.
- Misc helper types: `User`, `DeviceInfo`, `MissingResources`, `CheckpointInfo`, `TranslationPackage`, and `ClientFeatures`.

Main interactions:
- A concrete `Client` implementation will emit `ClientMessage` values (with `ClientEvent`) from `listen()` to inform callers of queue status, progress, and results.
- Callers enqueue jobs via `Client.enqueue(...)` (with the `front` flag for queue ordering), can interrupt a running job via `Client.interrupt()`, and can empty the server queue via `Client.clear_queue()`.

### Functions & Classes

`ClientEvent` — Enum
- Defines event kinds used in `ClientMessage`: `progress`, `finished`, `interrupted`, `error`, `connected`, `disconnected`, `queued`, `upload`, `published`, `output`, `payment_required`.
- Importance: central for queue/job lifecycle signaling; `queued` indicates when a job is placed in the server queue.

`TextOutput` — NamedTuple
- Fields: `key`, `name`, `text`, `mime`.
- Represents text-based outputs from a job (e.g., logs or textual results).

`SharedWorkflow` — NamedTuple
- Fields: `publisher`, `workflow`.
- Represents a published/shared workflow payload.

`ClientOutput` — type alias
- Union of `dict | SharedWorkflow | TextOutput`.
- Used as the `result` field type on `ClientMessage`.

`ClientMessage` — NamedTuple
- Fields: `event: ClientEvent`, `job_id: str`, `progress: float`, `images: ImageCollection | None`, `result: ClientOutput | None`, `error: str | None`.
- Importance: the message unit emitted by `Client.listen()` to notify the UI or caller about job progress, queue placement (`event == ClientEvent.queued`), results, and errors.

`User` — class (inherits `QObject`, `ObservableProperties`)
- Attributes: `id: str`, `name: str`, `images_generated = Property(0)`, `credits = Property(0)`.
- Signals: `images_generated_changed`, `credits_changed`.
- `__init__(self, id: str, name: str)` initializes a user.
- Importance: simple user model with observable properties — not directly queue-related but used for client state.

`DeviceInfo` — NamedTuple
- Fields: `type: str`, `name: str`, `vram: int`.
- `parse(data: dict)` (staticmethod): tries to extract device info from a server payload; logs on failure and returns a fallback.
- Importance: provides device capability info used for performance decisions (not queue logic, but relevant for resource/dispatch decisions).

`MissingResources` — Exception
- `__init__(self, missing: dict[Arch, list[ResourceId]] | list[CustomNode])` stores missing resources.
- `__str__(self)` returns a fixed message.
- `get(self, arch: Arch)` returns missing resources for a given arch (handles list vs dict storage).
- Importance: used by client implementations to report unavailable resources/models; may influence whether a job can be enqueued or should be blocked.

`CheckpointInfo` — NamedTuple
- Fields: `filename: str`, `arch: Arch`, `format: FileFormat = FileFormat.checkpoint`.
- Property `name` returns filename without ".safetensors".
- `deduce_from_filename(filename: str)` (staticmethod) constructs a `CheckpointInfo` with arch inferred.
- Importance: metadata about checkpoints for model selection; informs `ClientModels.arch_of`.

`ClientModels` — class
- Attributes described: `checkpoints: dict[str, CheckpointInfo]`, `vae: list[str]`, `loras: list[str]`, `upscalers: list[str]`, `resources: dict[str, str | None]`, `node_inputs: dict[str, dict[str, list[str | list | dict]]]`.
- `__init__(self)` initializes `node_inputs` and `resources`.
- `resource(self, kind: ResourceKind, identifier: ControlMode | UpscalerName | str, arch: Arch)` — constructs `ResourceId`, finds model via `find`, raises if not found.
- `find(self, id: ResourceId)` — lookup in `resources` with fallbacks: arch switching (e.g., illu_v → illu) and arch-agnostic search (`Arch.all`).
- `arch_of(self, checkpoint: str)` — returns arch from `checkpoints` if present, otherwise infers from name.
- `for_arch(self, arch: Arch)` — returns `ModelDict(self, ResourceKind.upscaler, arch)`.
- `upscale` (property) — returns `ModelDict(self, ResourceKind.upscaler, Arch.all)`.
- `default_upscaler` (property) — returns `resource(ResourceKind.upscaler, UpscalerName.default, Arch.all)`.
- Importance: central registry of which models/resources are available to the client. Resource lookup/fallback logic is essential when deciding whether a workflow can run (queue acceptance) and which files must be uploaded.

`ModelDict` — class
- `__init__(self, models: ClientModels, kind: ResourceKind, arch: Arch)` stores references.
- `__getitem__(self, key: ControlMode | UpscalerName | str)` — short-hand to `self._models.resource(self.kind, key, self.arch)`.
- `find(self, key: ControlMode | UpscalerName | str, allow_universal=False) -> str | None` — finds best matching model for key with special handling (e.g., composition/style map to reference for SD, universal fallback).
- `for_version(self, arch: Arch)` → `ModelDict(self._models, self.kind, arch)`.
- Properties: `text_encoder`, `clip_vision`, `upscale`, `control`, `ip_adapter`, `inpaint`, `lora`, `vae`, `fooocus_inpaint`, `all`, `node_inputs`, `has_te_vae`.
- Importance: a filtered view of `ClientModels` scoped by resource kind and architecture — used when constructing workflows or selecting resources for a queued job.

`TranslationPackage` — NamedTuple
- Fields: `code: str`, `name: str`.
- `from_dict(data: dict)` and `from_list(data: list)` helpers.
- Importance: encapsulates translation package metadata for `ClientFeatures`.

`ClientFeatures` — NamedTuple
- Fields: `ip_adapter: bool`, `translation: bool`, `languages: list[TranslationPackage]`, `max_upload_size: int`, `max_control_layers: int`, `wave_speed: bool`, `gguf: bool`.
- Importance: feature flags advertised by a server/client pair.

`Client` — abstract base class
- Attributes typed: `url: str`, `models: ClientModels`, `device_info: DeviceInfo`.
- Methods (abstract or overridable):
  - `connect(url: str, access_token: str = "") -> Client` (staticmethod, abstract, async) — connect to server and return a `Client`.
  - `enqueue(self, work: WorkflowInput, front: bool = False) -> str` (abstract, async) — enqueue a `WorkflowInput` on server; returns job id. The `front` boolean is provided to control queue placement (enqueue at front vs back).
  - `listen(self) -> AsyncGenerator[ClientMessage, Any]` (abstract) — async generator that yields `ClientMessage` updates (progress, queued, finished, etc.).
  - `interrupt(self)` (abstract, async) — interrupt currently running job on server.
  - `clear_queue(self)` (abstract, async) — instruct server to clear the job queue.
  - `refresh(self)` (async) — no-op default, override to refresh client state.
  - `translate(self, text: str, lang: str)` (async) — default returns input text, override to use server translation.
  - `disconnect(self)` (async) — default pass, override to close connection.
  - `user(self) -> User | None` (property) — default None.
  - `missing_resources(self) -> MissingResources | None` (property) — default None.
  - `supports_arch(self, arch: Arch) -> bool` — checks `missing_resources` for arch; returns True if supported.
  - `features(self) -> ClientFeatures` (property) — abstract.
  - `performance_settings(self) -> PerformanceSettings` (property) — abstract.
  - `__aenter__`, `__aexit__` async context manager helpers that call `disconnect`.
- Importance: This is the contract a concrete client must fulfill. Queue handling and mode logic must be implemented by concrete subclasses according to this interface. Key methods for queue handling are `enqueue`, `listen`, `interrupt`, and `clear_queue`; `enqueue` additionally exposes a `front` flag for queue ordering.

`resolve_arch(style: Style, client: Client | ClientModels | None = None)` — function
- Determines appropriate `Arch` to use for a `Style` given either a `Client` or `ClientModels` (or only the style). Uses `style.preferred_checkpoint` and model lookups to find checkpoint architecture. Honors user's explicit `style.architecture` for SDXL-like compatibility.
- Importance: determines which architecture a queued job should target (affects resource selection and whether a job is supported).

`filter_supported_styles(styles: Iterable[Style], client: Client | None = None)` — function
- Filters an iterable of `Style` objects to only those supported by `client` (based on `supports_arch` and presence of preferred checkpoint).
- Importance: UI-level filtering to avoid enqueuing unsupported jobs.

`loras_to_upload(workflow: WorkflowInput, client_models: ClientModels)` — generator function
- Collects Lora models referenced by a `WorkflowInput` (from `workflow.models.loras` and conditioning `region.loras`).
- Validates that each referenced Lora is available locally or already present on the server (`client_models.loras`). Raises `ValueError` with helpful messages if a required Lora is missing or if LCM-only Loras are referenced.
- Yields `lora_file` objects from the `FileLibrary` to be uploaded when needed.
- Importance: before enqueuing a job, implementation can call this to determine which Lora files need upload. Not directly queue-mode logic, but relevant to pre-enqueue validation and potential upload events (there is a `ClientEvent.upload` event too).

Constants:
- `_lcm_loras` — list of deprecated LCM Lora filenames.
- `_lcm_warning` — message advising users to change style sampling method.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
Agent's stated goal: "Find job queue handling and queue mode logic" and expects functions/classes related to job queue, enqueue/dequeue, queue mode values or flags like 'clear_queue' or 'replace'.

Relevant items in this file:
- `Client.enqueue(self, work: WorkflowInput, front: bool = False)` — primary enqueue operation; exposes the `front` boolean that controls whether the job should be placed at the front of the queue. Concrete client implementations will implement the actual enqueue semantics (including any replacement or queue-mode behaviors).
- `Client.listen(self) -> AsyncGenerator[ClientMessage, Any]` — yields `ClientMessage` objects that include queue-related `ClientEvent` values (notably `ClientEvent.queued`) and progress/status updates; this is how queue state is communicated back to callers.
- `Client.interrupt(self)` — allows interrupting currently running jobs (queue control).
- `Client.clear_queue(self)` — explicit abstract operation to clear the server queue (matches the expected 'clear_queue' flag/operation).
- `ClientEvent` — contains `queued` event and other lifecycle events used by queue handling and client status reporting.
- `ClientMessage` — carries `event` and `job_id` so callers can map server queue events to local job management and UI updates.

Notes on missing/absent items:
- There is no explicit `replace` flag or method in this file. The only queue-order control exposed here is the `front: bool` parameter on `enqueue`. Any "replace" semantics would need to be implemented by a concrete `Client` subclass (potentially by interpreting `front=True` together with server-side behavior), or by higher-level orchestration code not present in this file.
- Dequeue is not an explicit API here; job removal/management would be performed by `clear_queue`, `interrupt`, server-side APIs, or by the concrete `Client` implementation when interpreting incoming `ClientMessage` events.

If you want, I can scan other repository files (concrete Client implementations) to find how `enqueue(front=...)`, `ClientEvent.queued`, `clear_queue`, or any "replace" semantics are implemented server-side.

4. Highlight the parts most relevant to the agent's stated goal.

- Importance: simple user model with observable properties — not directly queue-related but used for client state.

`DeviceInfo` — NamedTuple
- Fields: `type: str`, `name: str`, `vram: int`.
- `parse(data: dict)` (staticmethod): tries to extract device info from a server payload; logs on failure and returns a fallback.
- Importance: provides device capability info used for performance decisions (not queue logic, but relevant for resource/dispatch decisions).

`MissingResources` — Exception
- `__init__(self, missing: dict[Arch, list[ResourceId]] | list[CustomNode])` stores missing resources.
- `__str__(self)` returns a fixed message.
- `get(self, arch: Arch)` returns missing resources for a given arch (handles list vs dict storage).
- Importance: used by client implementations to report unavailable resources/models; may influence whether a job can be enqueued or should be blocked.

`CheckpointInfo` — NamedTuple
- Fields: `filename: str`, `arch: Arch`, `format: FileFormat = FileFormat.checkpoint`.
- Property `name` returns filename without ".safetensors".
- `deduce_from_filename(filename: str)` (staticmethod) constructs a `CheckpointInfo` with arch inferred.
- Importance: metadata about checkpoints for model selection; informs `ClientModels.arch_of`.

`ClientModels` — class
- Attributes described: `checkpoints: dict[str, CheckpointInfo]`, `vae: list[str]`, `loras: list[str]`, `upscalers: list[str]`, `resources: dict[str, str | None]`, `node_inputs: dict[str, dict[str, list[str | list | dict]]]`.
- `__init__(self)` initializes `node_inputs` and `resources`.
- `resource(self, kind: ResourceKind, identifier: ControlMode | UpscalerName | str, arch: Arch)` — constructs `ResourceId`, finds model via `find`, raises if not found.
- `find(self, id: ResourceId)` — lookup in `resources` with fallbacks: arch switching (e.g., illu_v → illu) and arch-agnostic search (`Arch.all`).
- `arch_of(self, checkpoint: str)` — returns arch from `checkpoints` if present, otherwise infers from name.
- `for_arch(self, arch: Arch)` — returns `ModelDict(self, ResourceKind.upscaler, arch)`.
- `upscale` (property) — returns `ModelDict(self, ResourceKind.upscaler, Arch.all)`.
- `default_upscaler` (property) — returns `resource(ResourceKind.upscaler, UpscalerName.default, Arch.all)`.
- Importance: central registry of which models/resources are available to the client. Resource lookup/fallback logic is essential when deciding whether a workflow can run (queue acceptance) and which files must be uploaded.

`ModelDict` — class
- `__init__(self, models: ClientModels, kind: ResourceKind, arch: Arch)` stores references.
- `__getitem__(self, key: ControlMode | UpscalerName | str)` — short-hand to `self._models.resource(self.kind, key, self.arch)`.
- `find(self, key: ControlMode | UpscalerName | str, allow_universal=False) -> str | None` — finds best matching model for key with special handling (e.g., composition/style map to reference for SD, universal fallback).
- `for_version(self, arch: Arch)` → `ModelDict(self._models, self.kind, arch)`.
- Properties: `text_encoder`, `clip_vision`, `upscale`, `control`, `ip_adapter`, `inpaint`, `lora`, `vae`, `fooocus_inpaint`, `all`, `node_inputs`, `has_te_vae`.
- Importance: a filtered view of `ClientModels` scoped by resource kind and architecture — used when constructing workflows or selecting resources for a queued job.

`TranslationPackage` — NamedTuple
- Fields: `code: str`, `name: str`.
- `from_dict(data: dict)` and `from_list(data: list)` helpers.
- Importance: encapsulates translation package metadata for `ClientFeatures`.

`ClientFeatures` — NamedTuple
- Fields: `ip_adapter: bool`, `translation: bool`, `languages: list[TranslationPackage]`, `max_upload_size: int`, `max_control_layers: int`, `wave_speed: bool`, `gguf: bool`.
- Importance: feature flags advertised by a server/client pair.

`Client` — abstract base class
- Attributes typed: `url: str`, `models: ClientModels`, `device_info: DeviceInfo`.
- Methods (abstract or overridable):
  - `connect(url: str, access_token: str = "") -> Client` (staticmethod, abstract, async) — connect to server and return a `Client`.
  - `enqueue(self, work: WorkflowInput, front: bool = False) -> str` (abstract, async) — enqueue a `WorkflowInput` on server; returns job id. The `front` boolean is provided to control queue placement (enqueue at front vs back).
  - `listen(self) -> AsyncGenerator[ClientMessage, Any]` (abstract) — async generator that yields `ClientMessage` updates (progress, queued, finished, etc.).
  - `interrupt(self)` (abstract, async) — interrupt currently running job on server.
  - `clear_queue(self)` (abstract, async) — instruct server to clear the job queue.
  - `refresh(self)` (async) — no-op default, override to refresh client state.
  - `translate(self, text: str, lang: str)` (async) — default returns input text, override to use server translation.
  - `disconnect(self)` (async) — default pass, override to close connection.
  - `user(self) -> User | None` (property) — default None.
  - `missing_resources(self) -> MissingResources | None` (property) — default None.
  - `supports_arch(self, arch: Arch) -> bool` — checks `missing_resources` for arch; returns True if supported.
  - `features(self) -> ClientFeatures` (property) — abstract.
  - `performance_settings(self) -> PerformanceSettings` (property) — abstract.
  - `__aenter__`, `__aexit__` async context manager helpers that call `disconnect`.
- Importance: This is the contract a concrete client must fulfill. Queue handling and mode logic must be implemented by concrete subclasses according to this interface. Key methods for queue handling are `enqueue`, `listen`, `interrupt`, and `clear_queue`; `enqueue` additionally exposes a `front` flag for queue ordering.

`resolve_arch(style: Style, client: Client | ClientModels | None = None)` — function
- Determines appropriate `Arch` to use for a `Style` given either a `Client` or `ClientModels` (or only the style). Uses `style.preferred_checkpoint` and model lookups to find checkpoint architecture. Honors user's explicit `style.architecture` for SDXL-like compatibility.
- Importance: determines which architecture a queued job should target (affects resource selection and whether a job is supported).

`filter_supported_styles(styles: Iterable[Style], client: Client | None = None)` — function
- Filters an iterable of `Style` objects to only those supported by `client` (based on `supports_arch` and presence of preferred checkpoint).
- Importance: UI-level filtering to avoid enqueuing unsupported jobs.

`loras_to_upload(workflow: WorkflowInput, client_models: ClientModels)` — generator function
- Collects Lora models referenced by a `WorkflowInput` (from `workflow.models.loras` and conditioning `region.loras`).
- Validates that each referenced Lora is available locally or already present on the server (`client_models.loras`). Raises `ValueError` with helpful messages if a required Lora is missing or if LCM-only Loras are referenced.
- Yields `lora_file` objects from the `FileLibrary` to be uploaded when needed.
- Importance: before enqueuing a job, implementation can call this to determine which Lora files need upload. Not directly queue-mode logic, but relevant to pre-enqueue validation and potential upload events (there is a `ClientEvent.upload` event too).

Constants:
- `_lcm_loras` — list of deprecated LCM Lora filenames.
- `_lcm_warning` — message advising users to change style sampling method.

import asyncio
import json
import math
import os
import platform
import uuid
from base64 import b64encode
from copy import copy
from datetime import datetime
from dataclasses import dataclass
from itertools import chain

from .api import WorkflowInput
from .client import Client, ClientEvent, ClientMessage, ClientModels, DeviceInfo, CheckpointInfo
from .client import ClientFeatures, TranslationPackage, User, loras_to_upload
from .image import ImageCollection, qt_supports_webp
from .network import RequestManager, NetworkError
from .files import File
from .resources import Arch, ResourceKind, ControlMode, UpscalerName, resource_id
from .settings import PerformanceSettings, settings
from .localization import translate as _
from .util import clamp, ensure, client_logger as log
from . import resources, __version__ as plugin_version


@dataclass
class JobInfo:
    local_id: str
    work: WorkflowInput
    remote_id: str | None = None
    worker_id: str | None = None

    def __str__(self):
        return f"Job[{self.work.kind.name}, local={self.local_id}, remote={self.remote_id}]"


class CloudClient(Client):
    default_api_url = os.getenv("INTERSTICE_URL", "https://api.interstice.cloud")
    default_web_url = os.getenv("INTERSTICE_WEB_URL", "https://www.interstice.cloud")

    @staticmethod
    async def connect(url: str, access_token: str = ""):
        if not access_token:
            raise ValueError("Authorization missing for cloud endpoint")
        client = CloudClient(url)
        await client.authenticate(access_token)
        return client

    def __init__(self, url: str):
        self.url = url
        self.models = models
        self.device_info = DeviceInfo("Cloud", "Remote GPU", 24)
        self._requests = RequestManager()
        self._token: str = ""
        self._user: User | None = None
        self._current_job: JobInfo | None = None
        self._cancel_requested: bool = False
        self._queue: asyncio.Queue[JobInfo] = asyncio.Queue()
        self._features = enumerate_features({})

    async def _get(self, op: str):
        return await self._requests.get(f"{self.url}/{op}", bearer=self._token)

    async def _post(self, op: str, data: dict):
        return await self._requests.post(f"{self.url}/{op}", data, bearer=self._token)

    async def sign_in(self):
        client_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        info = f"Generative AI for Krita [Device: {platform.node()}]"
        log.info(f"Sending authorization request for {info} to {self.url}")
        init = await self._post("auth/initiate", dict(client_id=client_id, client_info=info))

        sign_in_url = f"{self.default_web_url}{init['url']}"
        log.info(f"Waiting for completion of authorization at {sign_in_url}")
        yield sign_in_url

        auth_confirm = await self._post("auth/confirm", dict(client_id=client_id))
        time = datetime.now()
        while auth_confirm["status"] == "not-found":
            if (datetime.now() - time).seconds > 300:
                raise TimeoutError(_("Sign-in attempt timed out after 5 minutes"))
            await asyncio.sleep(2)
            auth_confirm = await self._post("auth/confirm", dict(client_id=client_id))

        if auth_confirm["status"] == "authorized":
            self._token = auth_confirm["token"]
            log.info("Authorization successful")
            yield self._token
        else:
            error = auth_confirm.get("status", "unexpected response")
            raise RuntimeError(_("Authorization could not be confirmed: ") + error)

    async def authenticate(self, token: str):
        if not token:
            raise ValueError("Authorization missing for cloud endpoint")
        self._token = token
        try:
            user_data = await self._get(f"user?plugin_version={plugin_version}")
        except NetworkError as e:
            log.error(f"Couldn't authenticate user account: {e.message}")
            self._token = ""
            if e.status == 401:
                e.message = _("The login data is incorrect, please sign in again.")
            raise e
        self._user = User(user_data["id"], user_data["name"])
        self._user.images_generated = user_data["images_generated"]
        self._user.credits = user_data["credits"]
        self._features = enumerate_features(user_data)
        log.info(f"Connected to {self.url}, user: {self._user.id}")
        return self._user

    async def enqueue(self, work: WorkflowInput, front: bool = False):
        apply_limits(work, self.features)
        job = JobInfo(str(uuid.uuid4()), work)
        await self._queue.put(job)
        return job.local_id

    async def listen(self):
        yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.connected)
        while True:
            try:
                self._current_job = await self._queue.get()
                self._cancel_requested = False
                async for msg in self._process_job(self._current_job):
                    yield msg
                    if self._cancel_requested:
                        yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.interrupted, self._current_job.local_id)
                        break

            except NetworkError as e:
                job_id = self._current_job.local_id if self._current_job else ""
                if msg := self._process_http_error(e, job_id):
                    yield msg
                else:
                    msg = e.message
                    log.exception(f"Network error while processing {self._current_job}: {msg}")
                    if job_id:
                        yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.error, job_id, error=msg)
            except Exception as e:
                log.exception(f"Unhandled exception while processing {self._current_job}")
                if self._current_job is not None:
                    yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.error, self._current_job.local_id, error=str(e))
            except asyncio.CancelledError:
                break
            finally:
                self._current_job = None

    async def _process_job(self, job: JobInfo):
        user = ensure(self.user)
        inputs = job.work.to_dict(max_image_size=16 * 1024)

        async for progress in self.send_lora(job.work):
            yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.upload, job.local_id, progress)

        await self.send_images(inputs)

        data = {
            "input": {
                "workflow": inputs,
                "clientInfo": f"krita-ai-diffusion {plugin_version}",
                "options": {
                    "useWebpCompression": qt_supports_webp(),
                },
            }
        }
        response: dict = await self._post("generate", data)

        job.remote_id = response["id"]
        job.worker_id = response["worker_id"]
        cost = _update_user(user, response.get("user"))
        log.info(f"{job} started, cost was {cost}, {user.credits} tokens remaining")
        yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.progress, job.local_id, 0)

        status = response["status"].lower()
        while status == "in_queue" or status == "in_progress":
            response = await self._post(f"status/{job.worker_id}/{job.remote_id}", {})
            status = response["status"].lower()

            if status == "in_queue":
                yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.queued, job.local_id)

            elif status == "in_progress":
                progress = 0.09
                if output := response.get("output", None):
                    progress = output.get("progress", progress)
                yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.progress, job.local_id, progress)
            await asyncio.sleep(_poll_interval)

        if status == "completed":
            output = response["output"]
            images = await self.receive_images(output["images"])
            pose = output.get("pose", None)
            log.info(f"{job} completed, got {len(images)} images{', got pose' if pose else ''}")
            lora_warning = output.get("lora_warning", False)
            if lora_warning:
                log.warning(f"{job} encountered LoRA that could not be applied to the checkpoint")
            error = "incompatible_lora" if lora_warning else None
            yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.finished, job.local_id, 1, images, pose, error=error)

        elif status == "failed":
            err_msg, err_trace = _extract_error(response, job.remote_id)
            log.error(f"{job} failed\n{err_msg}\n{err_trace}")
            yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.error, job.local_id, error=err_msg)

        elif status == "cancelled":
            log.info(f"{job} was cancelled")
            yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.interrupted, job.local_id)

        elif status == "timed_out":
            log.warning(f"{job} timed out")
            msg = _("Generation took too long and was cancelled (timeout)")
            yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.error, job.local_id, error=msg)
        else:
            log.warning(f"Got unknown job status {status}")

    async def interrupt(self):
        if job := self._current_job:
            self._cancel_requested = True
            if job.remote_id and job.worker_id:
                response = await self._post(f"cancel/{job.worker_id}/{job.remote_id}", {})
                log.info(f"Requested cancellation of {job}: {response}")

    async def clear_queue(self):
        self._queue = asyncio.Queue()

    @property
    def user(self):
        return self._user

    @property
    def performance_settings(self):
        return PerformanceSettings(
            batch_size=clamp(settings.batch_size, 4, 8),
            resolution_multiplier=settings.resolution_multiplier,
            max_pixel_count=clamp(settings.max_pixel_count, 1, 8),
            dynamic_caching=False,
        )

    @property
    def features(self):
        return self._features

    async def send_images(self, inputs: dict, max_inline_size=4096):
        if image_data := inputs.get("image_data"):
            blob, offsets = image_data["bytes"], image_data["offsets"]
            if _base64_size(len(blob)) < max_inline_size:
                encoded = b64encode(blob).decode("utf-8")
                inputs["image_data"] = {"base64": encoded, "offsets": offsets}
            else:
                s3_object = await self._upload_image(blob)
                inputs["image_data"] = {"s3_object": s3_object, "offsets": offsets}

    async def _upload_image(self, data: bytes):
        upload_info = await self._post("upload/image", {})
        log.info(f"Uploading image input to temporary transfer {upload_info['url']}")
        await self._requests.put(upload_info["url"], data)
        return upload_info["object"]

    async def send_lora(self, workflow: WorkflowInput):
        for file in loras_to_upload(workflow, self.models):
            async for progress in self._upload_lora(file):
                yield progress

    async def _upload_lora(self, lora: File):
        assert lora.path and lora.hash and lora.size
        upload = await self._post("upload/lora", dict(hash=lora.hash, size=lora.size))
        if upload["status"] == "too-large":
            max_size = int(upload.get("max", 0)) / (1024 * 1024)
            raise ValueError(
                _("LoRA model is too large to upload") + f" (max {max_size} MB) {lora.name}"
            )
        if upload["status"] == "limit-exceeded":
            raise ValueError(_("Can't upload LoRA model, limit exceeded") + f" {lora.name}")
        if upload["status"] == "cached":
            return  # already uploaded
        log.info(
            f"Uploading LoRA model {lora.name} to cloud (hash={lora.hash}, url={upload['url']})"
        )
        try:
            data = lora.path.read_bytes()
            async for sent, total in self._requests.upload(upload["url"], data, sha256=lora.hash):
                yield sent / max(total, 1)
        except NetworkError as e:
            log.error(f"LoRA model upload failed [{e.status}]: {e.message}")
            raise Exception(_("Connection error during upload of LoRA model") + f" {lora.name}")
        except Exception as e:
            raise Exception(_("Error during upload of LoRA model") + f" {lora.name}") from e

    async def receive_images(self, images: dict):
        offsets = images.get("offsets")
        if not (isinstance(offsets, list) and len(offsets) > 0):
            raise ValueError(f"Could not read result images, invalid offsets: {offsets}")
        if url := images.get("url"):
            log.info(f"Downloading result images from temporary transfer {url}")
            data = await self._requests.download(url)
            return ImageCollection.from_bytes(data, offsets)
        elif b64 := images.get("base64"):
            return ImageCollection.from_base64(b64, offsets)
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"No result images found in server response: {str(images)[:80]}")

    async def compute_cost(self, input: WorkflowInput):
        response = await self._post("admin/cost", input.to_dict())
        return int(response.decode())

    def _process_http_error(self, e: NetworkError, job_id: str):
        if e.status == 402 and e.data and self.user:  # 402 Payment Required
            try:
                data = copy(e.data)
                data["url"] = f"{self.default_web_url}/user"
                self.user.credits = e.data["credits"]
                return ClientMessage(
                    ClientEvent.payment_required, job_id, result=e.data, error=e.message
                )
            except Exception:
                log.warning(f"Could not parse 402 error: {e.data}")
        return None


def _extract_error(response: dict, job_id: str | None):
    error = response.get("error", f'"Job {job_id} failed (unknown error)"')
    try:
        error_args = json.loads(error)
        err_msg = error_args.get("error_message", error_args)
        err_trace = error_args.get("error_traceback", "No traceback")
    except Exception:
        err_msg = str(error)
        err_trace = "No traceback"
    return err_msg, err_trace


def _update_user(user: User, response: dict | None):
    if response:
        cost = max(0, user.credits - response["credits"])
        user.images_generated = response["images_generated"]
        user.credits = response["credits"]
        return cost
    else:
        log.warning("Did not receive updated user data from server")
        return 0


def enumerate_features(user_data: dict):
    return ClientFeatures(
        ip_adapter=True,
        translation=True,
        languages=[
            TranslationPackage("zh", "Chinese"),
            TranslationPackage("fr", "French"),
            TranslationPackage("de", "German"),
            TranslationPackage("ru", "Russian"),
            TranslationPackage("es", "Spanish"),
        ],
        max_upload_size=user_data.get("max_upload_size", 300 * 1024 * 1024),
        max_control_layers=user_data.get("max_control_layers", 4),
    )


def apply_limits(work: WorkflowInput, features: ClientFeatures):
    if work.models:
        work.models.self_attention_guidance = False
    if work.conditioning:
        work.conditioning.control = work.conditioning.control[: features.max_control_layers]
        for region in work.conditioning.regions:
            region.control = region.control[: features.max_control_layers]
    if work.sampling:
        work.sampling.total_steps = min(work.sampling.total_steps, 1000)


def _base64_size(size: int):
    return math.ceil(size / 3) * 4


def _checkpoint_info(id: str, arch: Arch):
    models = chain(resources.default_checkpoints, resources.deprecated_models)
    res = next(m for m in models if m.id.identifier == id and m.arch == arch)
    return (res.filename, CheckpointInfo(res.filename, res.arch))


_poll_interval = 0.5  # seconds

models = ClientModels()
models.checkpoints = {
    filename: info
    for filename, info in (
        _checkpoint_info(name, arch)
        for name, arch in [
            ("dreamshaper", Arch.sd15),
            ("realistic_vision", Arch.sd15),
            ("serenity", Arch.sd15),
            ("flat2d_animerge", Arch.sd15),
            ("realvis", Arch.sdxl),
            ("zavychroma", Arch.sdxl),
            ("flux_schnell", Arch.flux),
            ("noobai", Arch.illu_v),
        ]
    )
}
models.vae = []
models.loras = [
    "Hyper-SD15-8steps-CFG-lora.safetensors",
    "Hyper-SDXL-8steps-CFG-lora.safetensors",
    "ip-adapter-faceid-plusv2_sd15_lora.safetensors",
    "ip-adapter-faceid-plusv2_sdxl_lora.safetensors",
]
models.upscalers = [
    "4x_NMKD-Superscale-SP_178000_G.pth",
    "HAT_SRx4_ImageNet-pretrain.pth",
    "OmniSR_X2_DIV2K.safetensors",
    "OmniSR_X3_DIV2K.safetensors",
    "OmniSR_X4_DIV2K.safetensors",
]
# fmt: off
models.resources = {
    resource_id(ResourceKind.controlnet, Arch.sd15, ControlMode.inpaint): "control_v11p_sd15_inpaint_fp16.safetensors",

tool_calls.result:
### File Overview
This file implements a concrete cloud-backed Client named `CloudClient` that communicates with a remote API to enqueue and process image-generation `WorkflowInput` jobs, handle authentication, upload/download assets (images, LoRA files), and report progress via `ClientMessage`/`ClientEvent`.

### Architecture
- Top-level: helper dataclass `JobInfo` and module-level utility functions (`_extract_error`, `_update_user`, `enumerate_features`, `apply_limits`, `_base64_size`, `_checkpoint_info`) and constants (`_poll_interval`, `models`).
- Main component: `CloudClient` (subclass of `Client`) encapsulates: authentication, a `RequestManager` for HTTP calls, an asyncio queue (`self._queue`) to hold `JobInfo` jobs, and the job processing lifecycle (`listen` -> `_process_job`).
- Job lifecycle: producers call `enqueue` to create a `JobInfo` and put it into `self._queue`. The consumer is `listen`, which loops awaiting `self._queue.get()`, sets `self._current_job`, runs `_process_job`, emits `ClientMessage` events for progress/queue/finished/error, and respects cancellation via `interrupt`.
- Upload/download: `send_lora` / `_upload_lora` handle LoRA uploads; `send_images` / `_upload_image` handle image input transfer; `receive_images` downloads or decodes result images.
- Features & limits: `enumerate_features` creates `ClientFeatures`; `apply_limits` enforces server-side client feature limits before enqueueing.

### Functions & Classes
- `JobInfo` — dataclass with fields `local_id: str`, `work: WorkflowInput`, `remote_id: str | None = None`, `worker_id: str | None = None`. __str__ returns a human-readable representation. Importance: encapsulates job identity and mapping between local and remote identifiers during processing.

- `CloudClient` — main client class (subclass of `Client`).
  - `default_api_url` (class attribute) — default cloud API URL.
  - `default_web_url` (class attribute) — default web UI URL.
  - `connect` (staticmethod) — `async def connect(url: str, access_token: str = "")`: creates a `CloudClient` instance and calls `authenticate` with the provided token. Importance: convenience factory for authenticated client creation.
  - `__init__` — sets up instance state: `self.url`, `self.models`, `self.device_info`, `self._requests` (RequestManager), `self._token`, `self._user`, `self._current_job`, `self._cancel_requested`, `self._queue` (an `asyncio.Queue[JobInfo]`) and `self._features`. Importance: constructs the in-memory queue and client state used by enqueue/listen.
  - `_get` — `async def _get(self, op: str)`: wrapper to GET `f"{self.url}/{op}"` via `RequestManager` with bearer token. Importance: HTTP GET helper.
  - `_post` — `async def _post(self, op: str, data: dict)`: wrapper to POST to `f"{self.url}/{op}"` via `RequestManager` with bearer token. Importance: HTTP POST helper.
  - `sign_in` — `async def sign_in(self)`: initiates a browser-based sign-in flow by calling `auth/initiate` and polling `auth/confirm`, yields the sign-in URL and finally yields the token when authorized. Importance: interactive sign-in coroutine used to obtain tokens.
  - `authenticate` — `async def authenticate(self, token: str)`: sets `self._token`, fetches user data via `_get("user?...")`, populates `self._user` and `self._features`. Importance: finalizes client authentication and loads user/features.
  - `enqueue` — `async def enqueue(self, work: WorkflowInput, front: bool = False)`: applies `apply_limits(work, self.features)`, creates `JobInfo(str(uuid.uuid4()), work)`, and `await self._queue.put(job)`, then returns `job.local_id`. Importance: primary producer API to queue a job for cloud processing. Notes: the `front` parameter exists in the signature but is not used anywhere in the method body (jobs are always enqueued via `asyncio.Queue.put()` at the back).
  - `listen` — `async def listen(self)`: main consumer loop. Yields `ClientMessage(ClientEvent.connected)` and then repeatedly:
    - `await self._queue.get()` to retrieve the next `JobInfo` (FIFO behavior).
    - sets `self._current_job`, resets `_cancel_requested`.
    - calls `_process_job(self._current_job)` and yields messages emitted by it.
    - if `_cancel_requested` becomes True, yields `ClientMessage(ClientEvent.interrupted, self._current_job.local_id)`.
    - handles `NetworkError` and general exceptions, mapping them to `ClientMessage` events using `_process_http_error` and logging.
    - finally clears `self._current_job`.
    Importance: drives the job execution pipeline, serializing job processing from the queue.
  - `_process_job` — `async def _process_job(self, job: JobInfo)`: core job processing:
    - ensures `user` exists and converts job inputs to dict.
    - uploads LoRAs via `send_lora`, yielding upload progress as `ClientMessage(ClientEvent.upload, job.local_id, progress)`.
    - calls `send_images(inputs)` to inline or upload image inputs.
    - posts `generate` request and records `job.remote_id` and `job.worker_id`.
    - updates user with `_update_user`.
    - yields initial progress `ClientEvent.progress`.
    - polls status via `_post(f"status/{job.worker_id}/{job.remote_id}", {})` in a loop while `status` is `in_queue` or `in_progress`:
      - when `in_queue` yields `ClientEvent.queued`;
      - when `in_progress` yields `ClientEvent.progress` with progress from response or a default.
    - on final statuses:
      - `completed`: downloads images via `receive_images`, yields `ClientMessage(ClientEvent.finished, job.local_id, 1, images, pose, error=error)`.
      - `failed`: extracts error via `_extract_error` and yields `ClientMessage(ClientEvent.error, job.local_id, error=err_msg)`.
      - `cancelled`: yields `ClientMessage(ClientEvent.interrupted, job.local_id)`.
      - `timed_out`: yields `ClientMessage(ClientEvent.error, job.local_id, error=msg)`.
    Importance: implements the entire lifecycle of a queued job interacting with the remote service and converting remote state to local `ClientMessage` events.
  - `interrupt` — `async def interrupt(self)`: if `self._current_job` exists, sets `self._cancel_requested = True` and, if `job.remote_id` and `job.worker_id` exist, posts a cancel to `f"cancel/{job.worker_id}/{job.remote_id}"`. Importance: allows cancellation of the currently processing job; sets a flag observed by `listen` to emit interruption events.
  - `clear_queue` — `async def clear_queue(self)`: replaces `self._queue` with a new `asyncio.Queue()`. Importance: clears all queued jobs by creating a new queue instance. Note: this does not signal or cancel a currently running job; it simply discards references to the old queue and its pending items.
  - `user` (property) — returns `self._user`.
  - `performance_settings` (property) — returns a `PerformanceSettings` instance computed from `settings` with clamps applied. Importance: exposes client performance constraints.
  - `features` (property) — returns `self._features`.
  - `send_images` — `async def send_images(self, inputs: dict, max_inline_size=4096)`: checks `inputs["image_data"]`, decides to inline as base64 if small enough or upload to temporary URL via `_upload_image`, then mutates `inputs["image_data"]` accordingly. Importance: prepares image payloads for `generate` requests.
  - `_upload_image` — `async def _upload_image(self, data: bytes)`: requests an upload URL from `upload/image`, PUTs bytes via `RequestManager`, returns S3 object identifier. Importance: uses temporary transfer mechanism for large image inputs.
  - `send_lora` — `async def send_lora(self, workflow: WorkflowInput)`: iterates over files returned by `loras_to_upload(workflow, self.models)` and yields progress from `_upload_lora`. Importance: coordinates uploading LoRA model files needed by the workflow.
  - `_upload_lora` — `async def _upload_lora(self, lora: File)`: posts `upload/lora` with hash and size, handles server responses (`too-large`, `limit-exceeded`, `cached`), and streams upload progress with `RequestManager.upload`. Importance: handles LoRA upload logic, error mapping, and yields upload progress fractions.
  - `receive_images` — `async def receive_images(self, images: dict)`: validates offsets, then either downloads via `url` or decodes base64 via `base64` data, returning an `ImageCollection`. Importance: reconstructs image outputs returned by the server.
  - `compute_cost` — `async def compute_cost(self, input: WorkflowInput)`: posts to `admin/cost` and returns parsed cost. Importance: query server cost estimation.
  - `_process_http_error` — `def _process_http_error(self, e: NetworkError, job_id: str)`: special-cases HTTP 402 (Payment Required) by updating `self.user.credits`, adding `url` for web UI, and returning `ClientMessage(ClientEvent.payment_required, job_id, result=e.data, error=e.message)` if parseable. Importance: maps specific HTTP errors into `ClientMessage` events consumable by UI.

- `_extract_error` — `def _extract_error(response: dict, job_id: str | None)`: parses `response["error"]` (possibly JSON) into `(err_msg, err_trace)`. Importance: extracts server-side error details for logs and client error reporting.

- `_update_user` — `def _update_user(user: User, response: dict | None)`: updates `user.images_generated` and `user.credits` if `response` present and returns computed cost; otherwise logs a warning and returns 0. Importance: maintains local user credit state in sync with the server.

- `enumerate_features` — `def enumerate_features(user_data: dict)`: builds and returns a `ClientFeatures` instance populated with translation packages and server limits like `max_upload_size` and `max_control_layers`. Importance: central place to translate server-reported capabilities into `ClientFeatures`.

- `apply_limits` — `def apply_limits(work: WorkflowInput, features: ClientFeatures)`: enforces client-side restrictions derived from `features` by disabling `self_attention_guidance` and truncating control layers & sampling steps. Importance: ensures workflow doesn't violate feature constraints before sending to server.

- `_base64_size` — `def _base64_size(size: int)`: computes size increase when base64-encoding bytes. Importance: used to decide inline vs. upload image strategy.

- `_checkpoint_info` — `def _checkpoint_info(id: str, arch: Arch)`: finds the matching checkpoint resource entry and returns `(filename, CheckpointInfo(...))`. Importance: populates `models.checkpoints`.

- Module-level variables:
  - `_poll_interval = 0.5` — seconds between status polls in `_process_job`.
  - `models = ClientModels()` with fields `checkpoints`, `vae`, `loras`, `upscalers`, and a `resources` mapping. Importance: describes default model/asset names and resource mapping used by upload/compatibility logic.

### Relevance to Agent's Goal
The agent is looking for the concrete client implementation handling enqueue and queue operations. Relevant findings:
- `CloudClient.enqueue` is the method that accepts a `WorkflowInput` and places a `JobInfo` into the queue via `await self._queue.put(job)`. It returns the generated `local_id`. The `front: bool = False` parameter exists but is unused — jobs are always enqueued to the back.
- The queue is `self._queue`, declared in `__init__` as `asyncio.Queue[JobInfo]`. Jobs are consumed by `listen`, which performs `self._current_job = await self._queue.get()` (FIFO behavior).
- To clear pending items, `CloudClient.clear_queue` replaces `self._queue` with a new `asyncio.Queue()` (effectively dropping references to pending jobs). There is no provision to insert jobs at the front of the queue, nor are there any queue mode flags or enums defined in this file.
- Cancellation interacts with the queue only indirectly: `interrupt` affects the currently processing job (`self._current_job`) by setting `_cancel_requested` and posting a cancel to the server when possible; it does not remove pending jobs from `self._queue`.
- The code emits lifecycle events via `ClientMessage`/`ClientEvent` during enqueue->process->finish, and uses `_poll_interval` for polling job status.

Summary for actions: if you need a "put to front" behavior, this file currently lacks it (the `front` argument is a no-op); to implement it you would need to change the queue implementation (e.g., use a deque or a custom queue supporting put_front) or adjust enqueue/listen logic. There are no queue mode flags/enums in this file.

## Source Code:
import asyncio
import json
import math
import os
import platform
import uuid
from base64 import b64encode
from copy import copy
from datetime import datetime
from dataclasses import dataclass
from itertools import chain

from .api import WorkflowInput
from .client import Client, ClientEvent, ClientMessage, ClientModels, DeviceInfo, CheckpointInfo
from .client import ClientFeatures, TranslationPackage, User, loras_to_upload
from .image import ImageCollection, qt_supports_webp
from .network import RequestManager, NetworkError
from .files import File
from .resources import Arch, ResourceKind, ControlMode, UpscalerName, resource_id
from .settings import PerformanceSettings, settings
from .localization import translate as _
from .util import clamp, ensure, client_logger as log
from . import resources, __version__ as plugin_version


@dataclass
class JobInfo:
    local_id: str
    work: WorkflowInput
    remote_id: str | None = None
    worker_id: str | None = None

    def __str__(self):
        return f"Job[{self.work.kind.name}, local={self.local_id}, remote={self.remote_id}]"


class CloudClient(Client):
    default_api_url = os.getenv("INTERSTICE_URL", "https://api.interstice.cloud")
    default_web_url = os.getenv("INTERSTICE_WEB_URL", "https://www.interstice.cloud")

    @staticmethod
    async def connect(url: str, access_token: str = ""):
        if not access_token:
            raise ValueError("Authorization missing for cloud endpoint")
        client = CloudClient(url)
        await client.authenticate(access_token)
        return client

    def __init__(self, url: str):
        self.url = url
        self.models = models
        self.device_info = DeviceInfo("Cloud", "Remote GPU", 24)
        self._requests = RequestManager()
        self._token: str = ""
        self._user: User | None = None
        self._current_job: JobInfo | None = None
        self._cancel_requested: bool = False
        self._queue: asyncio.Queue[JobInfo] = asyncio.Queue()
        self._features = enumerate_features({})

    async def _get(self, op: str):
        return await self._requests.get(f"{self.url}/{op}", bearer=self._token)

    async def _post(self, op: str, data: dict):
        return await self._requests.post(f"{self.url}/{op}", data, bearer=self._token)

    async def sign_in(self):
        client_id = str(uuid.uuid4())
        info = f"Generative AI for Krita [Device: {platform.node()}]"
        log.info(f"Sending authorization request for {info} to {self.url}")
        init = await self._post("auth/initiate", dict(client_id=client_id, client_info=info))

        sign_in_url = f"{self.default_web_url}{init['url']}"
        log.info(f"Waiting for completion of authorization at {sign_in_url}")
        yield sign_in_url

        auth_confirm = await self._post("auth/confirm", dict(client_id=client_id))
        time = datetime.now()
        while auth_confirm["status"] == "not-found":
            if (datetime.now() - time).seconds > 300:
                raise TimeoutError(_("Sign-in attempt timed out after 5 minutes"))
            await asyncio.sleep(2)
            auth_confirm = await self._post("auth/confirm", dict(client_id=client_id))

        if auth_confirm["status"] == "authorized":
            self._token = auth_confirm["token"]
            log.info("Authorization successful")
            yield self._token
        else:
            error = auth_confirm.get("status", "unexpected response")
            raise RuntimeError(_("Authorization could not be confirmed: ") + error)

    async def authenticate(self, token: str):
        if not token:
            raise ValueError("Authorization missing for cloud endpoint")
        self._token = token
        try:
            user_data = await self._get(f"user?plugin_version={plugin_version}")
        except NetworkError as e:
            log.error(f"Couldn't authenticate user account: {e.message}")
            self._token = ""
            if e.status == 401:
                e.message = _("The login data is incorrect, please sign in again.")
            raise e
        self._user = User(user_data["id"], user_data["name"])
        self._user.images_generated = user_data["images_generated"]
        self._user.credits = user_data["credits"]
        self._features = enumerate_features(user_data)
        log.info(f"Connected to {self.url}, user: {self._user.id}")
        return self._user

    async def enqueue(self, work: WorkflowInput, front: bool = False):
        apply_limits(work, self.features)
        job = JobInfo(str(uuid.uuid4()), work)
        await self._queue.put(job)
        return job.local_id

    async def listen(self):
        yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.connected)
        while True:
            try:
                self._current_job = await self._queue.get()
                self._cancel_requested = False
                async for msg in self._process_job(self._current_job):
                    yield msg
                    if self._cancel_requested:
                        yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.interrupted, self._current_job.local_id)
                        break

            except NetworkError as e:
                job_id = self._current_job.local_id if self._current_job else ""
                if msg := self._process_http_error(e, job_id):
                    yield msg
                else:
                    msg = e.message
                    log.exception(f"Network error while processing {self._current_job}: {msg}")
                    if job_id:
                        yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.error, job_id, error=msg)
            except Exception as e:
                log.exception(f"Unhandled exception while processing {self._current_job}")
                if self._current_job is not None:
                    yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.error, self._current_job.local_id, error=str(e))
            except asyncio.CancelledError:
                break
            finally:
                self._current_job = None

    async def _process_job(self, job: JobInfo):
        user = ensure(self.user)
        inputs = job.work.to_dict(max_image_size=16 * 1024)

        async for progress in self.send_lora(job.work):
            yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.upload, job.local_id, progress)

        await self.send_images(inputs)

        data = {
            "input": {
                "workflow": inputs,
                "clientInfo": f"krita-ai-diffusion {plugin_version}",
                "options": {
                    "useWebpCompression": qt_supports_webp(),
                },
            }
        }
        response: dict = await self._post("generate", data)

        job.remote_id = response["id"]
        job.worker_id = response["worker_id"]
        cost = _update_user(user, response.get("user"))
        log.info(f"{job} started, cost was {cost}, {user.credits} tokens remaining")
        yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.progress, job.local_id, 0)

        status = response["status"].lower()
        while status == "in_queue" or status == "in_progress":
            response = await self._post(f"status/{job.worker_id}/{job.remote_id}", {})
            status = response["status"].lower()

            if status == "in_queue":
                yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.queued, job.local_id)

            elif status == "in_progress":
                progress = 0.09
                if output := response.get("output", None):
                    progress = output.get("progress", progress)
                yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.progress, job.local_id, progress)
            await asyncio.sleep(_poll_interval)

        if status == "completed":
            output = response["output"]
            images = await self.receive_images(output["images"])
            pose = output.get("pose", None)
            log.info(f"{job} completed, got {len(images)} images{', got pose' if pose else ''}")
            lora_warning = output.get("lora_warning", False)
            if lora_warning:
                log.warning(f"{job} encountered LoRA that could not be applied to the checkpoint")
            error = "incompatible_lora" if lora_warning else None
            yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.finished, job.local_id, 1, images, pose, error=error)

        elif status == "failed":
            err_msg, err_trace = _extract_error(response, job.remote_id)
            log.error(f"{job} failed\n{err_msg}\n{err_trace}")
            yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.error, job.local_id, error=err_msg)

        elif status == "cancelled":
            log.info(f"{job} was cancelled")
            yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.interrupted, job.local_id)

        elif status == "timed_out":
            log.warning(f"{job} timed out")
            msg = _("Generation took too long and was cancelled (timeout)")
            yield ClientMessage(ClientEvent.error, job.local_id, error=msg)
        else:
            log.warning(f"Got unknown job status {status}")

    async def interrupt(self):
        if job := self._current_job:
            self._cancel_requested = True
            if job.remote_id and job.worker_id:
                response = await self._post(f"cancel/{job.worker_id}/{job.remote_id}", {})
                log.info(f"Requested cancellation of {job}: {response}")

    async def clear_queue(self):
        self._queue = asyncio.Queue()

    @property
    def user(self):
        return self._user

    @property
    def performance_settings(self):
        return PerformanceSettings(
            batch_size=clamp(settings.batch_size, 4, 8),
            resolution_multiplier=settings.resolution_multiplier,
            max_pixel_count=clamp(settings.max_pixel_count, 1, 8),
            dynamic_caching=False,
        )

    @property
    def features(self):
        return self._features

    async def send_images(self, inputs: dict, max_inline_size=4096):
        if image_data := inputs.get("image_data"):
            blob, offsets = image_data["bytes"], image_data["offsets"]
            if _base64_size(len(blob)) < max_inline_size:
                encoded = b64encode(blob).decode("utf-8")
                inputs["image_data"] = {"base64": encoded, "offsets": offsets}
            else:
                s3_object = await self._upload_image(blob)
                inputs["image_data"] = {"s3_object": s3_object, "offsets": offsets}

    async def _upload_image(self, data: bytes):
        upload_info = await self._post("upload/image", {})
        log.info(f"Uploading image input to temporary transfer {upload_info['url']}")
        await self._requests.put(upload_info["url"], data)
        return upload_info["object"]

    async def send_lora(self, workflow: WorkflowInput):
        for file in loras_to_upload(workflow, self.models):
            async for progress in self._upload_lora(file):
                yield progress

    async def _upload_lora(self, lora: File):
        assert lora.path and lora.hash and lora.size
        upload = await self._post("upload/lora", dict(hash=lora.hash, size=lora.size))
        if upload["status"] == "too-large":
            max_size = int(upload.get("max", 0)) / (1024 * 1024)
            raise ValueError(
                _("LoRA model is too large to upload") + f" (max {max_size} MB) {lora.name}"
            )
        if upload["status"] == "limit-exceeded":
            raise ValueError(_("Can't upload LoRA model, limit exceeded") + f" {lora.name}")
        if upload["status"] == "cached":
            return  # already uploaded
        log.info(
            f"Uploading LoRA model {lora.name} to cloud (hash={lora.hash}, url={upload['url']})"
        )
        try:
            data = lora.path.read_bytes()
            async for sent, total in self._requests.upload(upload["url"], data, sha256=lora.hash):
                yield sent / max(total, 1)
        except NetworkError as e:
            log.error(f"LoRA model upload failed [{e.status}]: {e.message}")
            raise Exception(_("Connection error during upload of LoRA model") + f" {lora.name}")
        except Exception as e:
            raise Exception(_("Error during upload of LoRA model") + f" {lora.name}") from e

    async def receive_images(self, images: dict):
        offsets = images.get("offsets")
        if not (isinstance(offsets, list) and len(offsets) > 0):
            raise ValueError(f"Could not read result images, invalid offsets: {offsets}")
        if url := images.get("url"):
            log.info(f"Downloading result images from temporary transfer {url}")
            data = await self._requests.download(url)
            return ImageCollection.from_bytes(data, offsets)
        elif b64 := images.get("base64"):
            return ImageCollection.from_base64(b64, offsets)
        else:
            raise ValueError(f"No result images found in server response: {str(images)[:80]}")

    async def compute_cost(self, input: WorkflowInput):
        response = await self._post("admin/cost", input.to_dict())
        return int(response.decode())

    def _process_http_error(self, e: NetworkError, job_id: str):
        if e.status == 402 and e.data and self.user:  # 402 Payment Required
            try:
                data = copy(e.data)
                data["url"] = f"{self.default_web_url}/user"
                self.user.credits = e.data["credits"]
                return ClientMessage(
                    ClientEvent.payment_required, job_id, result=e.data, error=e.message
                )
            except Exception:
                log.warning(f"Could not parse 402 error: {e.data}")
        return None


def _extract_error(response: dict, job_id: str | None):
    error = response.get("error", f'"Job {job_id} failed (unknown error)"')
    try:
        error_args = json.loads(error)
        err_msg = error_args.get("error_message", error_args)
        err_trace = error_args.get("error_traceback", "No traceback")
    except Exception:
        err_msg = str(error)
        err_trace = "No traceback"
    return err_msg, err_trace


def _update_user(user: User, response: dict | None):
    if response:
        cost = max(0, user.credits - response["credits"])
        user.images_generated = response["images_generated"]
        user.credits = response["credits"]
        return cost
    else:
        log.warning("Did not receive updated user data from server")
        return 0


def enumerate_features(user_data: dict):
    return ClientFeatures(
        ip_adapter=True,
        translation=True,
        languages=[
            TranslationPackage("zh", "Chinese"),
            TranslationPackage("fr", "French"),
            TranslationPackage("de", "German"),
            TranslationPackage("ru", "Russian"),
            TranslationPackage("es", "Spanish"),
        ],
        max_upload_size=user_data.get("max_upload_size", 300 * 1024 * 1024),
        max_control_layers=user_data.get("max_control_layers", 4),
    )


def apply_limits(work: WorkflowInput, features: ClientFeatures):
    if work.models:
        work.models.self_attention_guidance = False
    if work.conditioning:
        work.conditioning.control = work.conditioning.control[: features.max_control_layers]
        for region in work.conditioning.regions:
            region.control = region.control[: features.max_control_layers]
    if work.sampling:
        work.sampling.total_steps = min(work.sampling.total_steps, 1000)


def _base64_size(size: int):
    return math.ceil(size / 3) * 4


def _checkpoint_info(id: str, arch: Arch):
    models = chain(resources.default_checkpoints, resources.deprecated_models)
    res = next(m for m in models if m.id.identifier == id and m.arch == arch)
    return (res.filename, CheckpointInfo(res.filename, res.arch))


_poll_interval = 0.5  # seconds

models = ClientModels()
models.checkpoints = {
    filename: info
    for filename, info in (
        _checkpoint_info(name, arch)
        for name, arch in [
            ("dreamshaper", Arch.sd15),
            ("realistic_vision", Arch.sd15),
            ("serenity", Arch.sd15),
            ("flat2d_animerge", Arch.sd15),
            ("realvis", Arch.sdxl),
            ("zavychroma", Arch.sdxl),
            ("flux_schnell", Arch.flux),
            ("noobai", Arch.illu_v),
        ]
    )
}
models.vae = []
models.loras = [
    "Hyper-SD15-8steps-CFG-lora.safetensors",
    "Hyper-SDXL-8steps-CFG-lora.safetensors",
    "ip-adapter-faceid-plusv2_sd15_lora.safetensors",
    "ip-adapter-faceid-plusv2_sdxl_lora.safetensors",
]
models.upscalers = [
    "4x_NMKD-Superscale-SP_178000_G.pth",
    "HAT_SRx4_ImageNet-pretrain.pth",
    "OmniSR_X2_DIV2K.safetensors",
    "OmniSR_X3_DIV2K.safetensors",
    "OmniSR_X4_DIV2K.safetensors",
]
